event_world_42_20260423_084238_0000.name: Classical Gala Evening
event_world_42_20260423_084238_0000.description: The City Philharmonic performs Beethoven's 9th Symphony and Rachmaninoff's Piano Concerto No. 2 in a breathtaking candlelit hall.

reviews.text: Parking chaos added two hours to our evening and the toilet facilities were disgraceful.
The app kept showing wrong tent locations and staff gave contradictory directions.
Unforgettable night — the headline act played three encores and the crowd energy was incredible.
Electric atmosphere from start to finish — the performance was world-class.

reviews.text: Decent show but the venue's sightlines were poor from the back rows.

reviews.text: Decent event overall though the merchandise stall ran out of programmes by midday.
Overcrowded beyond safe limits; I had to leave early as the crowd pressure near the stage felt dangerous.
Average experience — neither the worst nor the best event I have attended this year.

event_world_42_20260423_084238_0040.name: Jazz Under the Stars
event_world_42_20260423_084238_0040.description: Grammy-winning trumpeter Marcus Cole leads a quartet through classic Coltrane, Miles Davis, and original compositions beneath an open sky.

reviews.text: The event started ninety minutes late with no explanation or apology from the organisers.
Confusing layout and poor signage made it hard to navigate between stages.
Performers were 90 minutes late and the cancellation announcement came via a single tweet.
The acoustic quality in the outer sections of the venue was noticeably worse than the floor.
The fireworks finale was extraordinary — timed perfectly to the closing score.

event_world_42_20260423_084238_0041.description: A boundary-pushing director stages Shakespeare's Hamlet in a dystopian near-future, with a gender-swapped cast and an original score by an electronic composer.

reviews.text: Accessible entrances were well signed and staff were genuinely helpful to our group member using a wheelchair.

event_world_42_20260423_084238_0042.name: Immersive Van Gogh
event_world_42_20260423_084238_0042.description: Walk inside the brushstrokes of van Gogh's masterworks as floor-to-ceiling 3D projections transform the gallery into Starry Night and Sunflowers.

event_world_42_20260423_084238_0043.name: Ancient Civilizations Exhibition
event_world_42_20260423_084238_0043.description: A stunning collection of artefacts on loan from the British Museum and the Louvre traces humanity's earliest cities — from Mesopotamia to the Indus Valley.

reviews.text: Decent event overall though the merchandise stall ran out of programmes by midday.

event_world_42_20260423_084238_0044.name: Classical Gala Evening
event_world_42_20260423_084238_0044.description: The City Philharmonic performs Beethoven's 9th Symphony and Rachmaninoff's Piano Concerto No. 2 in a breathtaking candlelit hall.

event_world_42_20260423_084238_0045.name: Artisan Crafts & Vintage Fair
event_world_42_20260423_084238_0045.description: Over 200 stalls of handmade jewellery, vintage clothing, rare books, and bespoke homeware from independent makers across the region.

reviews.text: Perfectly sized event — big enough to have scale but small enough to feel personal and accessible.

event_world_42_20260423_084238_0046.name: Electronic Beats Festival
event_world_42_20260423_084238_0046.description: Three stages, twelve DJs, and a laser show that runs all night. Top acts from Berlin's underground scene headline this pulse-pounding electronic showcase.
